_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: FL federal House batch-4: Mast (FL-21), Frankel (FL-22), Moskowitz (FL-23), plus FL-20 open-seat D candidates Manley and Douglas. Evidence-only; every TRUE/FALSE cited; nulls where no evidence. FL-20 context: Cherfilus-McCormick resigned 2026-04-21; no special election called; new map (signed 2026-05-04, upheld 2026-05-26/27) dismantles CD-20 but race proceeds on regular cycle (primary 2026-08-18).

profile.religion: Christian (evangelical; attends Calvary Chapel; shared testimony at TC3 Church FL)
profile.background: US Representative FL-21 (5th term); Army combat veteran (lost both legs in Afghanistan); chairs House Foreign Affairs Committee since Jan 2025. Heritage Action 84% (119th session), 77% lifetime. Running for re-election 2026 (primary Aug 18).
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from roll calls, cosponsorships, Heritage/SBA/NRA ratings, own statements. Key deviations: 2018 NYT op-ed backing an assault-weapons sales ban after Parkland (NRA '?' 2018, 'D' 2022, never retracted); July 2022 RFMA Yea (one of 47 Rs) before Dec flip on religious-liberty grounds; >$880K AIPAC/Israel-lobby money.

brian-mast.notes_set: Army combat veteran (lost both legs in Afghanistan). Chair, House Foreign Affairs Committee (119th). Heritage Action 84% session / 77% lifetime. SBA Pro-Life A+; Life at Conception Act cosponsor but holds rape/incest/life exceptions (restrictionist, not abolitionist). KEY DEVIATIONS: 2018 post-Parkland NYT op-ed called for banning future sales of 'assault or tactical firearms' + universal background checks — NRA downgraded him to 'D' (2022); no retraction found. Voted YES on Respect for Marriage Act July 2022 (one of 47 Rs), flipped to NO Dec 2022 citing religious liberty. One of Congress's largest AIPAC recipients; opposed June 2026 Iran war-powers resolution as HFAC chair. Jan 2021: objected to AZ/PA electors. Homeschool/school-choice advocate; cosponsored Parental Bill of Rights, CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act, Birthright Citizenship Act of 2025; Yea on SAVE Act; led USAID dismantlement oversight; fought military COVID vaccine mandate (Reenlistment Act).

0.note: Cosponsor, Life at Conception Act; SBA Pro-Life America A+.

1.note: Restrictionist, not abolitionist: holds rape/incest/life-of-mother exceptions; backs Pain-Capable (20-wk) and dismemberment bans — restriction bills.

lois-frankel.notes_set: Progressive Democrat; lead House sponsor of the Women's Health Protection Act (nationwide abortion access) and twice voted Nay on Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act. Equality Act cosponsor/Yea; Yea Respect for Marriage Act (Dec 2022); twice Nay on Protection of Women and Girls in Sports Act; Nay Parents Bill of Rights. Nay SAVE Act (2024+2025); Yea H.R. 1 (For the People). Nay Laken Riley Act (twice), Nay H.R. 30, Nay H.R. 2 Secure the Border Act. Yea 2022 assault-weapons ban; No on 2017 concealed-carry reciprocity; NRA F. EMILY's List was her #1 contributor (2012, $51,456); AIPAC/Israel-lobby career total $820,719 (TrackAIPAC) — AIPAC her #1 org contributor in 2022/2024/2026 cycles. CROSS-PARTY CREDIT: Aye on Yemen war-powers resolution (2019); Yea on 2002 AUMF repeal (2021). Heritage Action 0% session / 4% lifetime.

set.office: US Representative (FL-23; running 2026 in redrawn FL-25)

profile.religion: Jewish (public bios; cited antisemitic attacks on the Jewish community in his 2026 re-election announcement)
profile.background: US Representative FL-23 (since 2023); former Florida Division of Emergency Management director under DeSantis; co-author of the 2018 Marjory Stoneman Douglas Act as a state rep. First Democrat to join the DOGE Caucus. After Florida's May 2026 mid-decade redistricting, launched his 2026 re-election campaign in the newly drawn FL-25 (not a governor run). Heritage Action 6% (118th), ~3% lifetime.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized from House Clerk roll calls (verified in Clerk XML), cosponsor lists, ratings, endorsements. Genuine cross-party deviations on border enforcement, Israel/ICC, and government accountability; solidly progressive on life, LGBT policy, guns, and CBDC. Note: prior notes claimed Heritage 10-20% — actual is 6% (118th) / ~3% lifetime.

jared-moskowitz.notes_set: Moderate Democrat with documented cross-party deviations: YEA on Laken Riley Act twice (one of 48 Dems on H.R. 29) and H.R. 30; YEA censure of Rep. Tlaib (one of 22 Dems); YEA ICC-sanctions bills twice; YEA Israel aid w/ IRS offset (one of 12 Dems); AYE H.R. 357 agency-rulemaking accountability (one of 3 Dems); YEA blocking DC noncitizen voting; first Democrat in the DOGE Caucus. BUT: Reproductive Freedom for All 100%, PP Action Fund 2026 endorsee, Nay Born-Alive; Equality Act original cosponsor, twice Nay on women's-sports act, 'safeguarding marriage equality' platform; co-authored 2018 MSD Act (red-flag law + 21 age limit), NRA F- ('the lowest and proudest grade I've ever received'); voted NO on CBDC Anti-Surveillance State Act; Nay SAVE Act 2025. AIPAC his #1 contributor (TrackAIPAC career ~$779K). Heritage Action 6% (118th) / ~3% lifetime. Running 2026 in redrawn FL-25 after the May 2026 map.

set.office: U.S. Representative FL-20 (2026 D primary frontrunner · seat vacant since Cherfilus-McCormick resignation 2026-04-21)

profile.background: Teacher from Fort Lauderdale (Sistrunk), b. ~1999; raised in deep poverty by a single mother; B.A. History, UMD Global Campus (2025). Openly gay; self-described progressive in the AOC mold; as a teen sought the Socialist Party USA and Green Party 2016 presidential nominations (self-identified 'Democratic Socialist'); DSA-endorsed 2018 school-board run; lost FL House primaries 2020/2022. FL-20 frontrunner: Feb 2026 poll 38% vs. 35% Cherfilus-McCormick; ~$780K raised.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — evidence-finalized via the social-media protocol from his own campaign platform (elijahmanley.com issues pages), Ballotpedia Candidate Connection surveys (2018/2022/2025), endorsements, and press. Candidacy verified current: still running in the Aug 18, 2026 D primary (filing deadline June 12). Race volatility: seat vacant, no special election called, and the May 2026 map dismantles CD-20 — re-verify district status before the general.

elijah-manley.notes_set: 2026 FL-20 D primary frontrunner (Feb 2026 poll 38%, ~$780K raised) in a crowded field incl. Wasserman Schultz (redistricted in), Luther Campbell, Cherfilus-McCormick, Dale Holness. Seat vacant since SCM resigned 2026-04-21 ahead of a House Ethics hearing; DeSantis has not called a special election; the May 2026 mid-decade map dismantles CD-20 (upheld for 2026 on May 26-27 rulings). Platform (own site): codify Roe and 'vehemently oppose any attempt to limit reproductive rights'; openly gay, 'gender-affirming care is a right,' oppose all federal legislation targeting LGBTQ+ incl. right to marry; opposes diversion of public dollars to private schools; protect vote-by-mail, John Lewis VRA, same-day registration; pathway to citizenship, opposes deportation policies, reinstate TPS; 2018: 'beyond time to demand common sense gun reforms' (March for Our Lives figure); pro-NATO ('a vital force'), pro-Ukraine military aid, restore USAID, 2% of budget to foreign aid, rejoin Iran Deal — but anti-AIPAC (Track AIPAC + Peace Action endorsements; rejects corporate PAC money). CROSS-PARTY CREDIT: explicitly opposes a CBDC 'on privacy grounds' and backs decentralized crypto (own platform). Medicare for All, federal jobs guarantee, $20 minimum wage.

set.office: U.S. Representative FL-20 (2026 D Candidate · open seat · Cherfilus-McCormick vacancy)

profile.background: Mark A. Douglas — Jamaican-born litigation attorney (Mark A. Douglas, P.A., Fort Lauderdale); former two-term Sunrise City Commissioner (elected 2016; Deputy Mayor 2019, 2023); former Miami-Dade assistant state attorney; lost 2024 Sunrise mayoral race. J.D., FIU College of Law. FEC ID H6FL20085 (filed 2025-12-11); $0 raised/$0 spent — a paper candidacy.
profile.confidence_note: 2026-06-10 — full social-media-protocol sweep (FEC, Ballotpedia, FL DOS, campaign socials, local press) found ZERO federal policy positions in his own words; all cells null, nothing fabricated. CURRENCY FLAG: he does NOT appear on the Florida DOS candidate list for US Rep D-20 as of 2026-06-10; federal qualifying closes noon 2026-06-12 — if he doesn't qualify, mark not_running.

mark-douglas-fl-20.notes_set: Identity verified (distinct from the Tampa news anchor of the same name): Sunrise, FL attorney and former city commissioner; FEC Statement of Candidacy H6FL20085 filed Dec 11, 2025 for FL-20 (D); $0 raised. Listed on Ballotpedia for the Aug 18, 2026 D primary, but ABSENT from the FL Division of Elections candidate list for D-20 as of June 10, 2026 (qualifying window closes noon June 12, 2026 — re-verify ballot status after the deadline). No campaign website, questionnaire, or press quote on any federal issue found; old 2024 mayoral site is municipal-only. Zero scoreable positions — evidence-finalized with all cells null (roster/FEC-verified only).
